title: Automate configuring registry settings for Windows
content: This document explains how to create automations to configure registry settings on Windows devices in Hexnode. The Windows Registry is a central database that stores configuration data for the operating system, installed applications, user preferences, and hardware components. While the registry provides powerful control over system behaviour, manual edits are risky, time-consuming, and difficult to scale across an enterprise. Hexnode UEM enables administrators to automate registry configurations on Windows devices. They can specify the registry keys and values to be added, modified, or removed, and pair them with time schedules or activity-driven triggers. This ensures that edits are precise but also delivered to the right devices at the right time.

title: How to create an automation to configure registry settings?
content: Log in to Hexnode UEM. Navigate to theAutomatetab, clickNew Automation, and selectWindowsas the platform. After selecting the platform, click on theEditicon to theUntitled Automationand provide the following information to rename the automation profile. Automation Name: Enter a name for the automation. Description: Provide a brief description to clarify the automation intent or scope (optional). Once the details are provided, clickSave. Under Registry Configurations there are 2 actions,Registry EditorandRegistry Snapshot.

title: Registry Editor
content: Incorrectly editing the Windows Registry can cause serious system issues that require full OS reinstallation and potential data loss. To avoid this: Always create a registry backup or snapshot before applying any changes. Validate registry edits on a single device before deploying them across multiple systems. TheRegistry Editorallows you to define and deploy registry settings to your Windows devices. There are two ways to configure:Manual configurationandImport configuration. Manual configuration Use this option when you want to customize registry entries manually. You can select actions and enter the details for each action. Action: Start by selecting the type of operation you want to perform. There are 4 actions in manual registry configuration.Write Value Add or update a registry value.Delete Value Remove an existing registry value.Add Key Create a new registry key.Delete Key Remove an existing registry key. Write Value Add or update a registry value. Delete Value Remove an existing registry value. Add Key Create a new registry key. Delete Key Remove an existing registry key. Registry Path: After choosing the action, specify where in the registry the change should occur. Begin by selecting the root key for system-wide settings and then select the subkey path. Enter the remaining part of the path manually.Root key:HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINEHKEY_CURRENT_USERHKEY_CURRENT_CONFIGHKEY_USERSSub key: HARDWARE SAM SECURITY SOFTWARE SYSTEM Root key: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE HKEY_CURRENT_USER HKEY_CURRENT_CONFIG HKEY_USERS Sub key: HARDWARE SAM SECURITY SOFTWARE SYSTEM Value Name: Once the location is defined, provide the exact value to configure. The value name identifies the specific setting you want to modify under the chosen registry path. Data Type: , assign the type of data the value should hold. Supported types include:REG_BINARY Stores binary data in any form. For example,0001A4F2.REG_DWORD Stores 32-bit numeric values. For example,1(decimal) or 0x00000001(hexadecimal).REG_EXPAND_SZ Stores a variable-length string that can contain environment variables, which are expanded at runtime. For example USERNAME , expands to current username.REG_MULTI_SZ Stores multiple strings in a list. For example,00, 01, F2.REG_SZ Stores plain text strings. For example,C: Program Files AppName.REG_NONE Stores values with no defined data type.REG_QWORD Stores 64-bit numeric values. For example,9223372036854775807 (decimal) or 0x7FFFFFFFFFFFFFFF (hexadecimal). REG_BINARY Stores binary data in any form. For example,0001A4F2. REG_DWORD Stores 32-bit numeric values. For example,1(decimal) or 0x00000001(hexadecimal). REG_EXPAND_SZ Stores a variable-length string that can contain environment variables, which are expanded at runtime. For example USERNAME , expands to current username. REG_MULTI_SZ Stores multiple strings in a list. For example,00, 01, F2. REG_SZ Stores plain text strings. For example,C: Program Files AppName. REG_NONE Stores values with no defined data type. REG_QWORD Stores 64-bit numeric values. For example,9223372036854775807 (decimal) or 0x7FFFFFFFFFFFFFFF (hexadecimal). Value Type: For numeric data types of REG_DWORD and REG_QWORD, specify whether the number will be entered in Hexadecimal or Decimal format. Data: Finally, provide the actual data to be applied. If the chosen data type isREG_MULTI_SZ, separate each entry with a comma so that they are stored as multiple lines. Import configuration Use this option when you already have a registry configuration file (.reg) containing predefined settings. Upload the file to apply multiple registry changes in bulk without manually entering details.You can either: Select the file from the Hexnode repository. Or, Enter the local file path from which the .reg file will be fetched.

title: Registry Snapshot
content: This option takes a snapshot of the current device registry before making any changes. The snapshot will be stored underManage Device Device Info Registry. Creating a snapshot before applying edits is recommended, as it provides a backup that can later be referenced or restored to fix unwanted or faulty registry changes. Once the actions are selected and configured, click. Configure automation scheduling and related settings here. Trigger: The Trigger defines the condition that initiates the automation. Hexnode offers two types of triggers:TimeandActivity. Initiate: Set the automation initiation frequency. IfTimeis selected as the trigger, choose from: Once, ASAP Executes the automation immediately or as soon as possible. Once Executes the automation at a specific scheduled time. Repeat at a set schedule Executes the automation repeatedly according to a defined schedule (e.g.,Everday, Selected days, or Monthly). IfActivityis selected as the trigger, choose from: On Device Enrollment Triggers the automation action when a device is newly enrolled and completes its initial device scan. Devices that are already enrolled are unaffected, even if they meet the filter conditions. On Device Compliance Triggers the automation action when a device becomescompliantwith the defined policies. Devices that are already compliant remain unaffected. On Device Non-Compliance Triggers the automation action when a device becomes non-compliant. Devices that are already non-compliant remain unaffected. On Location Compliance Triggers the automation when a Windows device becomes location compliant. A device is considered location compliant when it is located within a geofence defined in Hexnode. Devices that are already location compliant remain unaffected. On Location Non-Compliance Triggers the automation when a Windows device becomes location non-compliant. Devices that are already location non-compliant remain unaffected. Once you have configured theSettings and Schedule, click. On the following page, you can define the target filters. Configure target filters in this section. Specify options forIncluded groups, Excluded groups, andcustom filters by selecting theFiltersoption. After setting the filters, click. Review the configured automation settings. ClickEditto modify any section if needed. Once you have reviewed the automation, clickSave. An automation runs only when both the trigger condition and the selected filters are satisfied.

title: How to keep track of the automation?
content: To view specific automation details, select the desired automation and navigate to theReportssection. Here, you can see device-specific details related to the automation, such as the device name, platform, action, version, initiation time, completion time, and the automation status. You can also export and download the report in either PDF or CSV format from this section.

title: What happens at the device after you configure registry settings?
content: When a registry automation is applied, the specified changes are made directly to the Windows Registry on the target device. Write Value: The value is added or updated at the specified registry path. Delete Value: The value is removed from the specified registry path. Add Key: A new registry key is created at the selected location. Delete Key: The specified registry key and all its values are removed. All changes take effect on the device according to the operating system or application behaviour. TheRegistry Snapshotwill be available as a.zip fileunderManage Device Device Info Registry. This can be used to refer and thereby restore the registry during any system failure. Share on: Share on:

title: Restrictions for Windows devices - Hexnode Help Center
content: Help Center Managing Windows Devices Restrictions Category filter

title: Restrictions for Windows devices
content: Configuring restrictions for Windows devices enforce control on how the users access these devices. You may allow or disallow Windows functionalities and features on the devices to ensure security to the organizational data and determine whether the corporate devices are utilized safely. Windows restriction policy can be used to generate restrictions based on device functionality, network connectivity, app configurations, security and privacy settings, and much more. The availability of the restrictions listed below depends on your UEM license plan and the Windows version of the end-user. For detailed information, please visit Hexnodepricing page. The restrictions mentioned below are supported on Pro, Business, Enterprise and Education editions.

title: Basic Restrictions
content: To configure basicRestrictionsfor Windows devices, Login to your Hexnode portal. Navigate toPolicies New Policyto create a new one or click on any policy name to edit an existing one. Enter thePolicy NameandDescriptionin the provided fields. Navigate toWindows Restrictions. Click onConfigure. Note that all the basic Windows restrictions in Hexnode areEnabledby default.

title: Allow Basic Device Functionality
content: On co-managed devices, storage cards will not be restricted. Click the dropdown to selectDisallow/ Limitedfor sending diagnostic data to Microsoft.Disallow If you chooseDisallow, diagnostic data will not be sent.Limited On choosingLimited, device can send only basic data to Microsoft. On Windows 11 devices, enabling other basic restrictions may not be effective unless Telemetry is configured to Limited or Disallow. Allow Users to Control(Default): When this option is selected, users can choose whether Windows apps can access the device s location. This setting can be configured by navigating toSettings Privacy and security Locationon the device. Force Location Off: When this option is selected, Windows apps will not be allowed to access the device s location. Force Location On: When this option is selected, Windows apps will be allowed to access the device s location. Telemetryis a feature in Windows where the system information will be sent to Microsoft to provide device-specific updates. Microsoft has already revealed that they usedtelemetryto count the number of times Alt Tab was used on a PC to switch between active Windows. They found that the number of users used Alt Tab were lesser since most of them were not familiar with that function, which then led to the addition ofTask Viewbutton in Windows 10. AutoPlaylets you choose the program with which you can start different kinds of media, such as DVD, CD, etc. containing music, video, photo, etc.AutoPlaybegins reading from a drive as soon as you insert media files in the drive. As a result, the setup file of programs and the music on audio media starts immediately.

title: Allow Basic App Settings
content: The device needs to be restarted for the restriction to take effect. On enabling Sync settings, Windows syncs all the settings you choose across all your Windows devices in which you have signed in with your Microsoft account. Sync settings also work if you sign in with a work or school account linked to your Microsoft account.

title: Allow Basic Security and Privacy Settings
content: If your device is Azure AD joined, disabling this option will have no effect.

title: Advanced Restrictions
content: To configure Advanced Restrictions for Windows devices, Login to your Hexnode UEM portal. Navigate toPolicies. You can either create a new policy or click on any policy name to edit an existing one. Enter thePolicy NameandDescriptionin the provided fields. Navigate toWindows Advanced Restrictions. Click onConfigure.

title: Allow Device Functionality
content: Allowed by default. Allowed by default. Allowed by default. Disabled by default. Regionoption is useful in finding localized content and apps. Allowed by default. Never First time Always Embedded moderestricts the device to run a single app (often calledkiosk mode). Embedded mode is allowed by default on devices running Windows 10 IoT Core. On mobile, and desktop devices, it must be enabled manually. Not only does this let you access a single app when using the device, Embedded Mode enables background tasks and other functionalities on the devices in addition to running single app in Kiosk mode.

title: File Explorer Settings
content: All folder locations Documents, Pictures, Downloads Desktop, Documents, Pictures, Downloads Desktop, Documents, Pictures, Downloads, Network This PC, Desktop, Documents, Pictures, Downloads This PC, Desktop, Documents, Pictures, Downloads, Network All storage locations Removable drives Sync roots Local drives Removable drives, sync roots Removable drives, local drives Sync roots, local drives Removable drives, sync roots, local drives

title: Allow Network Settings
content: All the Windows advanced network settings supported by Hexnode are allowed by default. Enabling this option deletes user-configured Wi-Fi and Wi-Fi sense profiles that have been ly installed on the device. Not all non-MDM profiles or non-user configured Wi-Fi profiles may get deleted completely. Wi-Fi Directis a certification from the non-profit Wi-Fi Alliance that allows devices to connect directly to each other without the need for a wireless router.

title: Allow Security and Privacy Settings
content: Disabling this option will prevent installation of provisioning package by run-time configuration agent. Allowed by default. Disabled by default. Allowed by default. Allowed by default. Allowed by default. Not Configured by default. Windows provisioning makes it easy for administrators to configure user devices without imaging. Aprovisioning package(.ppkg) is a container used for a collection of configuration settings. Provisioning packages can be installed using removable media such as an SD card or USB flash drive, attached to an email, downloaded from a network share, deployed in NFC tags or barcodes.

title: Customize Start Menu
content: For quick access, you can add different folders to show up on the left side , on Windows 10 devices. By default, only File Explorer and Settings folders will be listed there. The following restrictions allow Admin to customize start by choosing whether to show or hide shortcuts for some folders. Not Enforcedis selected as the default value for all the Start customization options. To add or remove the shortcuts from the Start , select the appropriate value from the drop-down. Drop-down values are:Hide shortcut/ Show shortcut. To add folders to the Windows 10 Start , Click onStart Settings. Click onPersonalization Start. Click onChoose which folders appear on Start. Click on the switch under the folder you want to add.

title: Advanced Account Settings
content: If this setting is configured, users cannot add a Microsoft account throughAccounts Other users Add account. If the user is logged in as a local account, the Sign in with a Microsoft account instead option underYour infowill be disabled. There are 3 options available: Not configured: No restrictions are applied. Users cannot add Microsoft accounts: Prevents users from adding new Microsoft accounts to their devices.NoteWhen this option is selected, users cannot switch from a local account to a Microsoft account or log out of an existing domain account and add a Microsoft account. When this option is selected, users cannot switch from a local account to a Microsoft account or log out of an existing domain account and add a Microsoft account. Users cannot add or log on with Microsoft accounts: Blocks users from adding new Microsoft accounts or logging in to existing ones. If this option is disabled, Users can add non-Microsoft accountsandUsers can connect using Microsoft accountsoptions are disabled. TheAdjust your photo,Account settings, andRelated settingsoptions under theAccounts Your Infosettings of the device are disabled. Additionally,Add account, andAccounts used by other appsoptions underAccounts Email Accountssettings are disabled. Users can add non-Microsoft accounts throughAccounts Email Accounts Add account. If this option is disabled, Users cannot use their Microsoft accounts to log in to non-mail-related apps like Microsoft Store, OneDrive, Microsoft Teams, etc. It also prevents setting a PIN for a new local user (even when created with a Microsoft account). If logged in as a local account, the users cannot sign in to a Microsoft account throughAccounts Your info Sign in with a Microsoft account instead. Users cannot add a Microsoft account fromAccounts Email Accounts Add a Microsoft account.

title: How to Apply the Restrictions to Devices/Groups?
content: There are two ways by which you can associate restrictions to the devices in bulk. If you haven t saved the policy yet, Navigate toPolicy Targets Click on Add Devices, and select the required device(s) to which you need to apply the policy ClickOK Click onSaveto apply the policies to the devices. To associate the policies with a device group, selectDevice Groupsfrom the left pane under Policy Targets, and follow the above instructions. Similarly, you can associate the policy withUsers,User Groups, orDomainsfrom the same pane. If you ve already saved the policy and you re taken to the page which displays the policy list, Select the required policy Click onManage Associate Targets SelectDevice/User/Device Group/User Group/Domain and select the device(s)/ user(s)/ device group(s)/ user group(s)/ domain(s) to which you need to apply the policy ClickAssociate.

title: Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
content: Hexnode UEM follows a most restrictive logic for policy arbitration. If one policy allows a feature (e.g., the Camera) and another restricts it, the device will enforce the restriction. This ensures that security is never compromised. Yes. For Windows devices, disassociating a policy or disenrolling the device from Hexnode UEM triggers an immediate lifting of restrictions. Yes. By unchecking Users can reset the device in the Advanced Restrictions section, Hexnode utilizes the Windows Policy CSP to disable the reset option within the Settings app, even for administrative accounts.

title: Troubleshooting
content: Probable Cause: The endpoint may lack the requisite registry entries used by the local Policy Manager to process management instructions. This condition is often the result of residual configurations from a MDM provider or corruption within the registry hives. Solution: Verify that the necessary registry keys are present on the device. For example, if the Allow SignIn Options restriction is non-functional, inspect the path:HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE SOFTWARE Microsoft PolicyManager default Settings . If keys are missing, you may need to re-enroll the device or use a Custom Script to manually re-inject the necessary registry values, allowing the UEM to interface effectively with the operating system s management architecture. Probable Cause: The local administrator account used to initially enroll the device has been deleted or renamed, leading to a state of Partial Enrollment . When the enrollment account is removed, the MDM profile is lost, which breaks communication for some Advanced Restrictions (like File Explorer settings). Solution: To resolve this, the user must navigate toSettings Accounts Access work or schooland re-authenticate the management account to reinstate the full MDM profile, or the user can re-enroll the device. Probable Cause: Persistence of the ExternallyManaged registry key from the management environment. Solution: Navigate to the registry pathHKLM Software Microsoft Enrollmentson the device. If theExternallyManagedkey is set to1, it may block the new MDM from applying restrictions. Set this value to0or delete the key, then initiate aScan Deviceaction from the Hexnode portal to re-enforce the policy. Share on: Share on:

title: How to configure website kiosk on Windows devices? - Hexnode Help Center
content: Help Center Kiosk Lockdown of Devices Windows Category filter

title: How to configure website kiosk on Windows devices?
content: This guide will take you through the steps to set up a website kiosk on Windows devices from Hexnode. A website kiosk restricts device access only to a specific website or a set of websites, ensuring a secure and controlled browsing experience. This is useful for customer service portals, online learning portals, and online exam portals, where restricting access to unauthorized content is essential. With Hexnode UEM, you can effortlessly configure a Windows website kiosk to limit users exclusively to necessary websites required for their tasks and prevent distractions while minimizing security risks associated with unrestricted browsing. In addition to specifying what the users can/cannot browse, you can enhance the website kiosk experience by customizing Kiosk Browser app settings. For the website kiosk, theKiosk Browser/Microsoft Edge app must be installed on the specific user account that has been set up to run in kiosk mode. This feature is supported on: Windows 10 Pro, Enterprise, and Education editions (version 1803 and later). Windows 11 Pro, Enterprise, and Education editions.

title: Steps to configure website kiosk on Windows
content: To configure a website kiosk on Windows devices via Hexnode UEM: Log in to your Hexnode UEM portal. Navigate toPolicies New Policy. Give a suitable name and description (optional) for the policy. You can also choose to continue with an existing policy. Navigate toKiosk Lockdown Windows Kiosk Lockdown Website Kiosk. ClickConfigure. Enter the name of the user account you want to run in website kiosk mode in theKiosk Account Namefield. If you are using the Microsoft account to enable sign in to the kiosk, you can specify the account name in either of the following formats:For an AD user:domain samAccountNameFor a Microsoft Entra ID user:email address For an AD user:domain samAccountName For a Microsoft Entra ID user:email address Under theBrowseroption, choose the desired browser (Kiosk Browser/Microsoft Edge) to be used while in kiosk mode. Provide the URL to be set as the default homepage when the kiosk mode is activated. In theNew Tab Page URLfield, enter the URL of the page that should open when the user opens a new tab in the browser (only applicable on Microsoft Edge browser). In theAdvanced Kiosk Settingssection, you can configure the following settings for the corresponding browsers:ForKiosk Browser:Enable forward/back : Enabling this option allows users to navigate forward and backward through webpages within the Kiosk Browser app. The buttons are located in the top left corner of the app.Enable Home Button: Enabling this option adds a home button to the Kiosk Browser app, alongside the buttons. When clicked, it redirects users to the default website kiosk home screen.Show Reset Button: Enabling this option adds a reset button to the top right corner of the Kiosk Browser app. As the user clicks the reset button, they will be prompted to confirm the closure of the website kiosk session. Once confirmed, all browsing data (such as cache and cookies) will be cleared, and the browser will return to the default URL home page.Browser Timeout: Allows you to set an idle time (between 1 and 1440 minutes) after which the Kiosk Browser app automatically restarts. A prompt appears 30 seconds before the timeout, giving the user the option to resume or end the session. If no action is taken, the browser resets automatically.ForMicrosoft EdgeSwipe gestures for forward/backward : Enabling this option allows users to navigate forward and backward in the browser using swipe gestures.Home button: Enabling this displays the home button on the browser, clicking on which redirects the user to the default home page.Users can modify URLs in the address bar: If enabled, users are allowed to modify the web address (URL) from the address bar for browsing. If disabled, the user will be restricted from modifying the URL.Delete downloaded files in the kiosk session upon exit: When enabled, all files downloaded during the kiosk session will be automatically deleted upon exit.Idle timeout: Allows you to set an idle time after which you can select the following idle timeout actions to take place. You can select one or more actions to happen when the device goes idle:Close browser: Closes the browser window.Clear browsing history: Clears the browser history.Clear download history: Clears the download history.Clear cookies and other site data: Clears the cookies and other site data.Clear cached images and files: Clears the cached images and files.Clear password Signin: Clears the saved sign-in passwords.Clear autofill: Clears the saved autofill data.Clear site settings: Clears the site settings.Reload pages: Reloads the web pages. ForKiosk Browser:Enable forward/back : Enabling this option allows users to navigate forward and backward through webpages within the Kiosk Browser app. The buttons are located in the top left corner of the app.Enable Home Button: Enabling this option adds a home button to the Kiosk Browser app, alongside the buttons. When clicked, it redirects users to the default website kiosk home screen.Show Reset Button: Enabling this option adds a reset button to the top right corner of the Kiosk Browser app. As the user clicks the reset button, they will be prompted to confirm the closure of the website kiosk session. Once confirmed, all browsing data (such as cache and cookies) will be cleared, and the browser will return to the default URL home page.Browser Timeout: Allows you to set an idle time (between 1 and 1440 minutes) after which the Kiosk Browser app automatically restarts. A prompt appears 30 seconds before the timeout, giving the user the option to resume or end the session. If no action is taken, the browser resets automatically. When clicked, it redirects users to the default website kiosk home screen. Show Reset Button: Enabling this option adds a reset button to the top right corner of the Kiosk Browser app. As the user clicks the reset button, they will be prompted to confirm the closure of the website kiosk session. Once confirmed, all browsing data (such as cache and cookies) will be cleared, and the browser will return to the default URL home page. A prompt appears 30 seconds before the timeout, giving the user the option to resume or end the session. If no action is taken, the browser resets automatically. If disabled, the user will be restricted from modifying the URL.Delete downloaded files in the kiosk session upon exit: When enabled, all files downloaded during the kiosk session will be automatically deleted upon exit.Idle timeout: Allows you to set an idle time after which you can select the following idle timeout actions to take place. You can select one or more actions to happen when the device goes idle:Close browser: Closes the browser window.Clear browsing history: Clears the browser history.Clear download history: Clears the download history.Clear cookies and other site data: Clears the cookies and other site data.Clear cached images and files: Clears the cached images and files.Clear password Signin: Clears the saved sign-in passwords.Clear autofill: Clears the saved autofill data.Clear site settings: Clears the site settings.Reload pages: Reloads the web pages. Swipe gestures for forward/backward : Enabling this option allows users to navigate forward and backward in the browser using swipe gestures. Home button: Enabling this displays the home button on the browser, clicking on which redirects the user to the default home page. Users can modify URLs in the address bar: If enabled, users are allowed to modify the web address (URL) from the address bar for browsing. If disabled, the user will be restricted from modifying the URL. Delete downloaded files in the kiosk session upon exit: When enabled, all files downloaded during the kiosk session will be automatically deleted upon exit. Idle timeout: Allows you to set an idle time after which you can select the following idle timeout actions to take place. You can select one or more actions to happen when the device goes idle:Close browser: Closes the browser window.Clear browsing history: Clears the browser history.Clear download history: Clears the download history.Clear cookies and other site data: Clears the cookies and other site data.Clear cached images and files: Clears the cached images and files.Clear password Signin: Clears the saved sign-in passwords.Clear autofill: Clears the saved autofill data.Clear site settings: Clears the site settings.Reload pages: Reloads the web pages. Close browser: Closes the browser window. Clear browsing history: Clears the browser history. Clear download history: Clears the download history. Clear cookies and other site data: Clears the cookies and other site data. Clear cached images and files: Clears the cached images and files. Clear password Signin: Clears the saved sign-in passwords. Clear autofill: Clears the saved autofill data. Clear site settings: Clears the site settings. Reload pages: Reloads the web pages. In theWebsite Kiosk URLssection, you can allowlist or blocklist URLs in kiosk mode. URLs can be added manually or uploaded via a CSV file. Manually added URLs can be edited or deleted later. To add a URL, enter the address along with the name and clickAdd.Allowlist: Add URLs that should be accessible in website kiosk mode.Blocklist: Add URLs that should be restricted in website kiosk mode.Notes:For specific websites, device and user info can be passed along with the URL to Windows devices. Include wildcards within the URL in the format:URL/wildcard.Example:https://www.wikipedia.org/ devicename / imei / serialnumber .The end user will only be able to access the specific URLs added in the website kiosk policy. For example, ifhttps://www.wikipedia.org/is allowlisted, the user will not be able to access its subdomains, such ashttps://en.wikipedia.org/. To allow access to all subdomains, consider allowlisting the URL containing the root domain (https://wikipedia.org/), i.e., without sub-domains.In kiosk mode, users are only allowed to download certain file types, such as.pdfor.jpg. However, this behavior can be modified by running acustom scripton the device to download additional file types.When using Microsoft Edge as the kiosk browser with URLs allowlisted, certain URLs must be explicitly allowed to ensure full browser functionality. For this, add edge:// to the allowlist under the kiosk configuration which will ensure access to all necessary URLs. Allowlist: Add URLs that should be accessible in website kiosk mode. Blocklist: Add URLs that should be restricted in website kiosk mode. For specific websites, device and user info can be passed along with the URL to Windows devices. Include wildcards within the URL in the format:URL/wildcard.Example:https://www.wikipedia.org/ devicename / imei / serialnumber . Example:https://www.wikipedia.org/ devicename / imei / serialnumber . The end user will only be able to access the specific URLs added in the website kiosk policy. For example, ifhttps://www.wikipedia.org/is allowlisted, the user will not be able to access its subdomains, such ashttps://en.wikipedia.org/. To allow access to all subdomains, consider allowlisting the URL containing the root domain (https://wikipedia.org/), i.e., without sub-domains. In kiosk mode, users are only allowed to download certain file types, such as.pdfor.jpg. However, this behavior can be modified by running acustom scripton the device to download additional file types. When using Microsoft Edge as the kiosk browser with URLs allowlisted, certain URLs must be explicitly allowed to ensure full browser functionality. For this, add edge:// to the allowlist under the kiosk configuration which will ensure access to all necessary URLs. After configuring the website kiosk settings, navigate toPolicy Targetsto apply the policy to devices, groups, users, or domains. ClickSaveto apply the policy.

title: What happens at the device end?
content: Once the user (specified in theKiosk Account Namefield) logs in, the device will automatically launch the Kiosk Browser/Microsoft Edge app and enter Website Kiosk mode. In this mode, the device will display the default website specified in the configured policy, limiting access to any other apps or functionalities.

title: How to exit kiosk mode?
content: You can exit devices from kiosk mode either by disassociating or archiving the policy. Besides, you also need to restart the device to remove it from kiosk mode.

title: Method 1: Disassociate the policy
content: Log in to the Hexnode UEM portal. Navigate to thePoliciestab. Select the desired policy. Go toPolicy Targets. Click onRemoveon the right side of the device. Log in to the Hexnode UEM portal. Navigate to theManagetab. Click on the device from which the policy needs to be disassociated. This will take you to theDevice summarypage. Go toPolicies. Identify the policy andclickon thetrashicon to the policy.

title: Method 2: Archive the policy
content: Log in to the Hexnode UEM portal. Navigate to thePoliciestab. Select the desired policy. Click onManage Move to Archive. Log in to the Hexnode UEM portal. Navigate to theManagetab. Click on the device from which the policy needs to be disassociated. This will take you to theDevice summarypage. Go toPolicies. Select the policy. Click onManage Move to Archive. The archived policies can be viewed underPolicies Archived Policies. Topermanently deletean archived policy,Log in to the Hexnode UEM portal.Navigate toPolicies Archived Policies.Select the desired policy. Click onManage Delete Confirmdeletion. Log in to the Hexnode UEM portal. Navigate toPolicies Archived Policies. Select the desired policy. Click onManage Delete Confirmdeletion. Torestorean archived policy,Log in to the Hexnode UEM portal.Navigate toPolicies Archived Policies.Select the desired policy. Click onManage Restore. Log in to the Hexnode UEM portal. Navigate toPolicies Archived Policies. Select the desired policy. Click onManage Restore. On restoring an archived policy, the policy targets won t be restored (the policy stays disassociated from the target device).

title: Method 3: Exit from the device end
content: If the above methods fail, pressCTRL ALT DEL. This locks the screen and allows users to sign in with a different account from the login page. However, the user account remains in kiosk mode, and once the user logs in to the account, the kiosk mode gets relaunched. Share on: Share on:

title: How to exit kiosk mode on Windows devices? - Hexnode Help Center
content: Help Center Kiosk Lockdown of Devices Windows Category filter

title: How to exit kiosk mode on Windows devices?
content: Kiosk mode is a feature used to lock down a device into displaying and running policy specified app(s). Hexnode UEM provisions two types of kiosk modes for Windows devices Single Appkiosk andMulti Appkiosk. Single app kiosk enables only a specified app to run in the kiosk. Multi app kiosk locks the Windows 10/11 PC on the Start , set in full-screen mode. Kiosk apps are added to the Start , and the user is restricted to using only those apps. Listed below are the various methods to exit kiosk mode on your Windows device.

title: Method 1: Disassociate the device/user from Policy Targets
content: Navigate to thePoliciestab. Find and open your kiosk policy. Go to thePolicy Targetssection on the same page. Identify the policy targets you want to disassociate the policy from and clickremove. The policy target may be a device, user, device group, user group, or domain. Save the policy to deploy the changes. If you wish to remove all associations, click onRemove AllunderPolicy Targets.

title: Method 2: Disassociate policy from the device or user
content: Navigate to theManagetab. for your device under theDevicessection and click on the device name to open the device details page. Navigate to thePoliciessection on the same page. You ll find your kiosk policy listed here.Click on the bin icon beside the kiosk policy. If the policy is associated with a device/user group, the group has to be reconfigured to exclude the devices you want to disassociate or the whole group has to be disassociated. In such cases, it will show the mapping status under thePoliciessub-tab as Device group, and you won t find the bin icon to remove it. Navigate to theManagetab. Select theUserssection. for the user and click on the username to open the user details page. Navigate to thePoliciessection on the same page. You ll find your kiosk policy listed here. Click on the bin icon beside the kiosk policy. If the policy is associated with a user group and you want to disassociate a specific user, the user has to be removed from the user group, or the whole group has to be disassociated. In this case, it will show the mapping status under the Policies sub-tab as a user group and you won t find the bin icon to remove it. Navigate to theManagetab. Select theUser Group/Device Groupsection. for the group and select the group name to open the group details page. Navigate to thePoliciessection on the same page. You ll find your kiosk policy listed here. Click on the bin icon beside the kiosk policy.

title: Method 3: Archive the policy
content: Navigate to thePoliciestab. for the associated kiosk policy and mark the checkbox to the left of the corresponding policy title. Click on theManagedropdown ( just above the policies list) and selectMove to Archive. Selecting this option will move the policy to the Archived policies section and automatically disassociate the ly associated devices. You may choose to restore or delete the policy later from the Archived Policies tab.

title: Method 1: Pressing Ctrl + Alt + Del keys
content: Pressing the Ctrl, Alt, and Del keys simultaneously on your kiosk device while inside the kiosk account will prompt the user to sign out. The user will be redirected to the login page from where they may log in as a different user.

title: Method 2: Signing out the kiosk user
content: Multi app kiosk opens the Start in full screen with the policy specified app pinned as tiles. To exit, click the user icon from the Start options and selectSign Out. Unfortunately, this method won t work in single app kiosk mode on windows devices. Please remember that both methods will only sign out the user from the kiosk account to display the login screen. Kiosk mode will be resumed once the user logs into this account again. On shut down or restart, the device will automatically sign in to the kiosk user account. Share on: Share on:

title: How to configure Windows Digital Signage Display? - Hexnode Help Center
content: Help Center Kiosk Lockdown of Devices Windows Category filter

title: How to configure Windows Digital Signage Display?
content: Digital signage is a versatile solution that enables businesses to display multimedia content on electronic screens. It is widely used for dynamic brand marketing and advertising. But beyond advertising, it also serves multiple purposes. Digital displays can provide instructions to help visitors navigate efficiently in hospitals and subways. In retail environments, they showcase product information and promotions, while in corporate settings, forming an effective medium for sharing company news and updates. IT admins can now seamlessly deploy customized and dynamic signage content across their Windows devices. This document offers a step-by-step guide to remotely configuring and managing digital signage on these devices. For the digital signage kiosk to function, theKiosk Browserapp must be installed on the specific user account that has been set up to run in kiosk mode. This feature supports the following file types: Images: JPEG and PNG Videos: MP4 and MOV Websites: URLs beginning with http or https are recommended Supported on: Windows 10 Pro, Enterprise, and Education editions (version 1803 and later). Windows 11 Pro, Enterprise, and Education editions.

title: Set up digital signage display in Windows kiosk mode
content: To configure Windows digital signage display via Hexnode UEM, Login to your Hexnode portal. Navigate toPolicies. Select an existing policy or create a new one by clickingNew Policy. Provide a policy name and a suitable description. FromKiosk Lockdown Windows Kiosk Lockdown, selectDigital Signage Displayand clickConfigure. Enter the name of the user account you want to run in digital signage kiosk mode in theKiosk Account Namefield. If you are using the Microsoft account to enable sign in to the kiosk, you can specify the account name in either of the following formats:For an AD user:domain samAccountNameFor a Microsoft Entra ID user:email address For an AD user:domain samAccountName For a Microsoft Entra ID user:email address Use the following options to set up the digital signage display content,Add URL: Choose this option to display the website of your choice. Enter a URL in the respective field in the following format https://www.hexnode.com/ .Note:For specific websites, device and user information can be passed along with the URL to devices. Configure wildcards in the URL using the format: myURL/ wildcard . The following wildcards are supported: devicename phonenumber deviceid model email serialnumber name assettag domain devicenotes netbiosname department For example: myURL/ devicename / name / serialnumber .Add File: Choose this option to display a photo, video, or a document of your choice. Choose a file from your system and upload it to the Hexnode content repository or select an already existing file. Add URL: Choose this option to display the website of your choice. Enter a URL in the respective field in the following format https://www.hexnode.com/ .Note:For specific websites, device and user information can be passed along with the URL to devices. Configure wildcards in the URL using the format: myURL/ wildcard . The following wildcards are supported: devicename phonenumber deviceid model email serialnumber name assettag domain devicenotes netbiosname department For example: myURL/ devicename / name / serialnumber . For specific websites, device and user information can be passed along with the URL to devices. Configure wildcards in the URL using the format: myURL/ wildcard . The following wildcards are supported: devicename phonenumber deviceid model email serialnumber name assettag domain devicenotes netbiosname department For example: myURL/ devicename / name / serialnumber . Add File: Choose this option to display a photo, video, or a document of your choice. Choose a file from your system and upload it to the Hexnode content repository or select an already existing file.

title: Advanced Kiosk Settings
content: The following advanced settings will provide administrators with some additional options to configure the digital signage display. Fit to Full-Screen: Check this option to stretch the content to fill the entire screen, regardless of its original aspect ratio. Note that videos, images, and PDFs may be cropped if their aspect ratio does not match the device s display. Loop Video: Enable this option to play video files in a continuous loop.

title: Associate the policy to target devices
content: If the policy has not been saved, Navigate toPolicy Targets Add Devices. Choose the target devices and clickOK. ClickSave. You can also associate the policy with device groups, users, user groups or domains from the left pane of the Policy Targets tab. If the policy has been saved, Go to Policies tab and choose the desired policy. Click onManagedrop-down and selectAssociate Targets. Choose the target entities and click Associate .

title: What happens at the device end?
content: Once the policy is successfully associated, the kiosk account on the device enters digital signage mode. The configured URLs, images, or videos will be displayed dynamically on the screen. To exit digital signage, users can log out of the kiosk account by pressingWin Shift Del. Share on: Share on:

title: A complete guide for Windows kiosk mode - Hexnode Help Center
content: Help Center Kiosk Lockdown of Devices Windows Category filter

title: Getting started
content: Windows Kiosk Lockdown mode aims at creating a confined environment in which Windows devices can be configured for specific objectives. Implementing Kiosk Lockdown in Windows devices is very easy, and it provides additional features such as kiosk-specific restrictions, resulting in increased security when compared to the normal mode.

title: System requirements
content: To activate Windows kiosk mode, the system requires AWindows 10device withPro,Enterprise, orEducationeditions running onv1709or later, orWindows 11device withPro,EnterpriseorEducationeditions. An active internet connection.

title: Enrolling Windows devices with Hexnode UEM
content: Enrolling your Windows device with Hexnode UEM is the first step in setting up kiosk mode on the device. This establishes a connection between the device and Hexnode UEM. At the end of the enrollment procedure, the Hexnode UEM app will get automatically installed on the Windows device. To enroll the device in Hexnode UEM, log in as administrator on the Windows device. The device can be enrolled via the Hexnode Installer app. It is supported on the following versions:Windows 10 v1709 or below, ifVisual C Redistributableand.NET framework version 4.7.2or higher are installed on the Windows device.Windows 10 v1803 or higher.Windows 11. Windows 10 v1709 or below, ifVisual C Redistributableand.NET framework version 4.7.2or higher are installed on the Windows device. Windows 10 v1803 or higher. Windows 11. If the Visual C Redistributable or .NET framework is not installed in the Windows device that is to be enrolled via the Hexnode Installer app, an error message will be shown asking the user to install the missing dependencies. When OK is clicked, the user will be redirected to a Microsoft developer website from where the package can be downloaded and installed. Ensure that the package chosen matches the system architecture and OS compatibility of the Windows device. There are different types of enrollments, each of which is explained below: Open Enrollment Authenticated EnrollmentSelf-EnrollmentEmail or SMS Enrollment Self-Enrollment Email or SMS Enrollment PPKG enrollment Google Workspace Enrollment for Windows

title: Under theEnrolltab, choosePlatform Specific > Windows > Windows PCs & Tablets.Ensure that theOpen Enrollmentpage has opened, if not, selectOpen Enrollment.Pick aDefault Userand enter aDefault Password.Choose theOwnershipmode required for the device: eitherCorporateorPersonaland clickNext. These settings define if the devices enrolled in Hexnode are corporate-owned or personal.Note:For enabling users to enroll devices on their own, the best method is to send them the enrollment request containing the enrollment URL and instructions for device enrollment. This request can be sent by navigating toEnroll > Settings > Request Modesand selectingEmailorSMS. Then, under theManagetab, select the users from theUserssubtab and clickActions > New Enrollment.
content: Under theEnrolltab, choosePlatform Specific Windows Windows PCs Tablets. Ensure that theOpen Enrollmentpage has opened, if not, selectOpen Enrollment. Pick aDefault Userand enter aDefault Password. Choose theOwnershipmode required for the device: eitherCorporateorPersonaland click. These settings define if the devices enrolled in Hexnode are corporate-owned or personal. For enabling users to enroll devices on their own, the best method is to send them the enrollment request containing the enrollment URL and instructions for device enrollment. This request can be sent by navigating toEnroll Settings Request Modesand selectingEmailorSMS. Then, under theManagetab, select the users from theUserssubtab and clickActions New Enrollment.

title: Enrolling the device via Open Enrollment
content: To enroll a Windows device via open enrollment, either of the following methods can be used: Open the web browser on the Windows device to be enrolled. Type in the Hexnode enrollment URL which will be in the formathttps://yourportal.hexnodemdm.com/enroll/. ClickDownload, thus initiating the Hexnode Installer app download on the Windows device. Open the app on the device and selectYeson the Hexnode Installer Setup wizard to allow the app to make changes to the device. Now, clickInstall. After reading the EULA agreement on the Hexnode Installer app, click onAgree and Enroll. The Hexnode Installer checks for the enrollment authentication settings with the portal and then processes the enrollment request. If the process succeeds, go to step 8. If the enrollment request process fails,Click onEnroll. The user will now be redirected toSettings Accounts Access Work or School Enroll in Device Management.InSet up a work or school account, the admin s username and the enrollment server address will be auto-filled. Now click on.After reading the instructions concerning the device setup, clickGot it. A connection will now be established between Hexnode and Workplace or School. All the apps and configurations that the organization has set up for the user will soon be pushed to the device. If the user doesn t get access to these after waiting for a few minutes, navigate toSettings Accounts Access Work or school Infoand clickSync. Click onEnroll. The user will now be redirected toSettings Accounts Access Work or School Enroll in Device Management. InSet up a work or school account, the admin s username and the enrollment server address will be auto-filled. Now click on. After reading the instructions concerning the device setup, clickGot it. A connection will now be established between Hexnode and Workplace or School. All the apps and configurations that the organization has set up for the user will soon be pushed to the device. If the user doesn t get access to these after waiting for a few minutes, navigate toSettings Accounts Access Work or school Infoand clickSync. Now, the Hexnode UEM app will get installed on the device, thereby applying all configurations to the device. Click Done to exit the Hexnode Installer. Also, click on Finish to exit the Setup Wizard. Enrollment via Hexnode Installer app is favored if the device is Windows 10 v1803 or a higher version. If the user is unable to install dependency packages on the devices running on Windows 10 v1709 or lower versions, the Native device enrollment method is recommended. On the Windows device, navigate toSettings Accounts Access work or school. ChooseEnroll only in device managementand enter the work mail ID. Click. Close the tab that now appears asking for the Microsoft password. Now type in the server URL which will be in the formathttps://yourportal.hexnodemdm.com/enroll/and click. After reading the instructions concerning the device setup, clickGot it. The device has now been successfully enrolled in the Hexnode UEM portal. A connection will now be established between Hexnode and Workplace or School. All the apps and configurations that the organization has set up for the user will soon be pushed to the device. If the user doesn t get access to these after waiting for a few minutes, navigate toSettings Accounts Access Work or school Infoand clickSync.

title: Configuring Authenticated Enrollment on the portal
content: There are two methods by which authenticated enrollment can be configured on the Hexnode portal. The steps are as follows. In this method, an enrollment request consisting of the enrollment instructions and the server URL for enrolling the devices is sent to users via Email or SMS. Under theEnrolltab, choosePlatform Specific Windows Windows PCs Tablets. Click onSwitch to Authenticated Enrollment Authenticated Enrollment. Choose the type of users (AD/ Microsoft Entra ID/ Local/ Google/ Okta) to be enrolled via theEnrollment Request. Pick the Ownership type:Personal,Corporate,Let the user choose, orChoose it later.Let the user chooseenables the user to choose the ownership type during the enrollment andChoose it laterlets the admin choose the ownership type at a later step. Now, click. Choose eitherEmail, orSMSor both, depending on how the enrollment request has to be sent. Change theDomainand select the respectiveUser. ClickSend. Under theEnrolltab, choosePlatform Specific Windows Windows PCs Tablets. Click onSwitch to Authenticated Enrollment Authenticated Enrollment. Choose the type of users (AD/ Microsoft Entra ID/ Local/ Google/ Okta) to be enrolled via theEnrollment Request. Pick the Ownership type:Personal,Corporate,Let the user choose, orChoose it Later. Click. To enroll the device via directory authentication, configureAD,Microsoft Entra ID,Google, orOktadirectory in the Hexnode portal.

All the apps and configurations that the organization has set up for the user will soon be pushed to the device. If the user doesn t get access to these after waiting for a few minutes, navigate toSettings Accounts Access Work or school Infoand clickSync. Click onEnrollto enroll the device. The user will now be redirected toSettings Accounts Access Work or School Enroll in Device Management. InSet up a work or school account, the admin s username and the enrollment server address will be auto-filled. Now click on. After reading the instructions regarding the device setup, clickGot it. A connection will now be established between Hexnode and Workplace or School. All the apps and configurations that the organization has set up for the user will soon be pushed to the device. Now, the Hexnode UEM app will get installed on the device, thereby applying all configurations to the device. ClickDoneto exit the Hexnode Installer. Also, click onFinishto exit the Setup Wizard. On the Windows device, navigate toSettings Accounts Access work or school. ChooseEnroll only in device managementand enter the work mail ID. Click. Close the tab that now appears asking for the Microsoft password. Now type in the server URL which will be in the formathttps://yourportal.hexnodemdm.com/enroll/and click. title: PPKG enrollment
content: PPKG enrollmentis a swift and smooth method by which administrators can set up the settings and configurations for enrollment. This method has various advantages it being a one-time setup and also facilitating efficient bulk enrollment of devices.

title: Google Workspace enrollment for Windows
content: The integration of Hexnode with Google Workspace helps organizations to enroll their Windows devices with the configured Google Workspace account. ForGoogle Workspace enrollment of Windows devices, the company must first configure its Google Workspace account in the Hexnode UEM portal. By syncing the users with the portal, enrollment requests can be sent to the users or self-enrollment can be done. Hexnode authenticates the users by their corresponding Google Workspace usernames and passwords.

title: App installation
content: Kiosk mode can be put to use not just for the applications currently present in the device, but also for new apps pushed and installed via Hexnode. This section deals with installing applications on the Windows device via Hexnode.Hexnode supports an assortment of app types. Apps can be added to the Hexnode app inventory and later deployed to devices, or they can even be pushed directly from Microsoft Store.

title: Adding Microsoft Store apps to Hexnode app inventory
content: In the Hexnode UEM portal, navigate to theAppstab. From the Appsdropdown, selectStore App. Uncheck theiOS,Android, andmacOScheckboxes. Type the name of the required app in the box and click. To narrow down the apps for a specific country, click onSelect Country. Click onAddto select the appropriate app. The selected apps will be successfully added to the Hexnode app inventory.

title: Deploying Microsoft Store apps to Windows devices
content: Microsoft Store apps can be distributed to Windows devices from the Hexnode portal in two ways: Install via Policy Install via Actions Kiosk mode is only applicable to Universal Windows Platform apps, i.e., apps pre-installed on the Windows device or sourced from Microsoft Store, and to Windows desktop apps like MSI, and Exe apps. Microsoft Store apps can be deployed and installed on Windows devices using theMandatory Appspolicy in the Hexnode portal. Login to the Hexnode UEM portal and navigate to thePoliciestab. ClickNew Policyor edit an existing policy. In case of a new policy, enter the policy name and description in the respective field. SelectWindows App Management Mandatory Apps Configure. Click Addand chooseAdd ApporAdd Group. The required application can be ed either fromLocal Appsor directly from thePublic Store. Once the app is selected, clickDone. On selectingAdd Group, and select the required apps similarly and clickDone. Navigate to thePolicy Targetssubtab and associate the policy to the required Devices/Device Groups/Users/User Groups/Domains andSavethe policy.

title: Method I
content: In the Hexnode UEM portal, navigate to theManagetab and select the required devices. FromActions, selectInstall Application. UncheckiOS,Android,macOS, andtvOScheckboxes inLocal Apps, and and select the required app. Apps can be directly installed fromPublic Storealso. Once the required app is selected, clickDone.

title: Method II
content: In the Hexnode UEM portal, navigate to theManagetab and select the required device, thereby moving to the device summary page of the corresponding device. FromActions, selectInstall Application. UncheckiOS,Android,macOS, andtvOScheckboxes inLocal Apps, and and select the required app. Apps can be directly installed fromPublic Storealso. Once the required app is selected, clickDone.

title: Configuring Kiosk Lockdown on Windows devices
content: Once the required apps are installed on the device, the users can follow further steps to implement Windows kiosk mode by creating a local user account on the device and pushing the Single App/Multi App kiosk policy to the device. Both the Single App kiosk and Multi App kiosk in Windows are supported on Windows 10 Pro, Enterprise, and Education editions running on versions 1709 or higher, and on Windows 11 Pro, Enterprise and Education editions. Kiosk can be enforced only on a local user account.

title: Creating a Local user account on a Windows device
content: Click on theStartbutton Settings Accounts Family and other users. UnderOther users, selectAdd someone else to this PC. Click onI don t have this person s sign-in informationand selectAdd a user without a Microsoft account. Fill in the username, password, and other necessary fields of the kiosk account. Click on theStart button Settings Accounts Other people. SelectAdd someone else to this PC. ChooseUsersfrom the inset box. Under Actions, click onUsers More actions New User. Fill in the username, password, and other necessary fields of the kiosk account. Now, the standard local user account will be set up on the Windows device. Click on theStartbutton. Navigate toSettings Accounts Other users. Select the optionAdd account. In the inset box, selectUsers. Under Actions, selectUsers More actions New User. Fill in the kiosk user sname,password, and other required fields. Now, the standard local user account will be set up on the Windows device.

title: How to create a Single App kiosk policy
content: This feature locks down the Windows device to a single chosen application. All other apps are restricted from functioning on the device. With Hexnode, a single app kiosk can be configured to run an application in full screen inside a restricted local user account. To enforce Windows Kiosk Lockdown mode on the device, the kiosk policy must be pushed to the device. The following steps describe how to push a single app kiosk policy to Windows devices: In the Hexnode UEM portal, navigate to thePoliciestab. SelectNew Policyand provide it with a suitable Policy name and description. Under theKiosk Lockdownsubtab, selectWindows Kiosk Lockdown. ChooseSingle Appand clickConfigure. Add the required app in kiosk mode by clicking on the button. Navigate to thePolicy Targetssubtab and associate the policy to the required Devices/Device Groups/Users/User Groups/Domains andSavethe policy. The policy will be automatically associated with the selected devices. When the user logs in to the kiosk account (local user account), the device automatically launches into kiosk mode and the designated app opens in full screen. The users will be restricted from accessing the desktop, start , settings, or any other apps on the device. Learn more the single app kiosk mode

title: Configuring a Multi App kiosk policy
content: In Multi App kiosk mode, the device is locked down with access only given to a limited number of whitelisted applications. The users will be restricted from accessing other features or apps on the device thus reducing distractions and providing users with exclusively what they need to access. Once the local user account has been created on the Windows device and the required apps have been installed, a start layout consisting of the apps to be included in the kiosk needs to be created. As mentioned earlier, customizing the start layout implies the process of arranging the apps involved in kiosk mode as per how the user wishes to view the apps on the start and the device screen. The Windows device in which the Start layout (XML or JSON) is set up should have the same OS version as that of the devices to which the kiosk policy is deployed. Check theRun as administratoroption if a prompt appears asking to enable it (specific to certain folders). To customize and export the Start layout as per user requirement, follow the steps given below: Login to the admin account andPin apps to Start. From theStart, right-click on the required app and selectPin to Start. For the apps that are not to be displayed in the layout, right-click on the app and selectUnpin from Start. Drag tiles to group the apps. Now, right-click onStartand selectWindows PowerShell. For devices running Windows 10 version 1607, 1703, or 1803, enter the commandExport-StartLayout path .xmlin Windows PowerShell, where-pathsignifies the path and file name of the XML file to be exported.For example,Export-StartLayout -path C:/Users/David/KioskLayout.xml. For devices running Windows 10 version 1809 or above, enter the commandExport-StartLayout -UseDesktopApplicationID path.xmlin Windows powershell, where-pathsignifies the path and file name of the XML file to be exported. For example,Export-StartLayout -UseDesktopApplicationID -path C: Users Robert Kiosk.xml For devices running Windows 11, enter the commandExport-StartLayout path.jsonin Windows powershell, where pathsignifies the path and file name of the JSON file to be exported. For example,Export-StartLayout -path C: Users Robert Kiosk.json The file name should include the .xml or .json extension. The policy settings require the extensions and the Export-StartLayout cmdlet does not append the file name extension. Ensure that the apps to be added to the kiosk are already present on the local user account. If an app is present in the Start layout but isn t added to the kiosk policy, it cannot be accessed when kiosk mode is launched on the device. For the policy to take effect, the device has to be restarted. When the user logs in to the kiosk account (local user account), the apps added in the kiosk mode will be shown on the start . The users will be restricted from accessing settings or other apps on the device. Learn more the multi app kiosk mode

title: Disabling kiosk mode
content: In the Hexnode UEM portal, navigate to thePoliciestab. Click on the kiosk policy and under theManagedropdown, selectMove to Archive. When a policy is archived, it will be moved toArchived Policies, and the policy targets will be removed automatically. In the Hexnode UEM portal, navigate to thePoliciestab. Click on the kiosk policy and underPolicy Targets, select theremoveoption corresponding to the device. In the Hexnode UEM portal, navigate to theManagetab. Select the required device and navigate toPolicies. Click on the trash can icon corresponding to the kiosk policy.

title: Reports
content: Hexnode UEM allows you to generate various reports that help in analyzing information associated with different devices and users. You can access a host of reports, covering a wide range of fields including Device Reports, User Reports, Compliance Reports, Location Reports, Application Reports,Audit Reports, etc. These can be accessed from theReportstab in the Hexnode portal. This data can be exported and saved in PDF or CSV format. The following kiosk reports can be generated: Kiosk active devices: It is a list of all devices that are currently locked down in kiosk mode. Kiosk enabled devices: It is a list of all devices to which the kiosk policy has been pushed, but are not currently in kiosk mode. Kiosk exited devices: It is a list of all devices that have exited the kiosk mode. Share on: Share on:

title: How to lock down Windows devices to a single app kiosk mode? - Hexnode Help Center
content: Help Center Kiosk Lockdown of Devices Windows Category filter

title: How to lock down Windows devices to a single app kiosk mode?
content: Kiosk mode is a lockdown mechanism that allows you to restrict your mobile devices to a single app or a handful of applications of your choice. Hexnode UEM helps you configure single app kiosk mode on your Windows 10/11 PCs to run a Universal Windows Platform (UWP) app in full screen inside a restricted local user account. WindowsSingle App Kioskpolicy is supported only on:Windows 10 Pro (running v1709 or later),EnterpriseandEducationeditions.Windows 11Pro,EnterpriseandEducationeditions. Windows 10 Pro (running v1709 or later),EnterpriseandEducationeditions. Windows 11Pro,EnterpriseandEducationeditions.

title: Configure single app kiosk
content: Before associating the policy, you must create a local user account on your device and install the app you want the user to be able to run. The kiosk account should necessarily be a local standard user account. The single app kiosk policy can also be applied to an Active Directory user account, provided the user has logged in to the device at least once before applying the kiosk policy. Once the user logs in to the kiosk user account, thekiosk mode statusand the name of thekiosk accountwill be displayed underEnrollment detailson theDevice Summary page.

title: Step 1: Create a Local user account on your Windows device
content: Select theStartbutton. ChooseSettings Accounts Family other people. Select the optionAdd someone else to this PCunderOther people. Click on the linkI don t have this person s sign-in information. Select the optionAdd a user without a Microsoft account. Fill in the kiosk user sname,password, and other required fields. Select theStartbutton. ChooseSettings Accounts Other people. Select the optionAdd someone else to this PC. In the inset box, selectUsers. UnderActions, selectUsers More actions New User. Fill in the kiosk user sname,password, and other required fields. Select theStartbutton. ChooseSettings Accounts Family other users. Select the optionAdd account toAdd other user. Click on the linkI don t have this person s sign-in information. Select the optionAdd a user without a Microsoft account. Fill in the kiosk user sname,password, and other required fields. Now, the user account will be set up on the device.

title: Step 2: Install the kiosk app within the local account (kiosk account)
content: Log into the machine using the local user account that you have created and install the app from Microsoft Store if the app is not already present on the account. Kiosk mode works only with Windows Store apps/UWP apps/Desktop apps.

title: Step 3: Create a single app kiosk policy
content: Log in to your Hexnode UEM portal. Navigate to thePoliciestab. Click onNew Policyto create a new one or click on any policy name to edit an existing one. Enter thePolicy NameandDescriptionin the provided fields. Go toKiosk Lockdown FromWindows Kiosk Lockdown, selectSingle App Configure. Choose eitherUWP/Windows Store App or Desktop Appsto add to the single app kiosk.For UWP/Windows Store App:Enter theKiosk Account Name. This can be either:Astandard local user account, orAMicrosoft account(including Active Directory or Microsoft Entra ID users)If you re using a Microsoft account for kiosk sign-in, specify the account in one of the following formats:AD user: domain samAccountName (e.g., hexnode alexanderj)Microsoft Entra ID user: email address.On the device, the user will be able to sign in to the kiosk account using their Active Directory or Microsoft Entra ID credentials.Click the button to select the app to be added in kiosk mode.For Desktop Apps:Notes:This feature is not available on Windows Pro devices.Enable theAutologonaccount option to automatically create a new standard local account named Kiosk and sign in to it after a device restart once the policy is associated.Enter theKiosk Account Name. This can be either:Astandard local user account, orAMicrosoft account(including Active Directory or Microsoft Entra ID users)If you re using a Microsoft account for kiosk sign-in, specify the account in one of the following formats:AD user: domain samAccountName (e.g., hexnode alexanderj)Microsoft Entra ID user: email address.On the device, the user will be able to sign in to the kiosk account using their Active Directory or Microsoft Entra ID credentials.Notes:If theAutologon accountoption is enabled, theKiosk Account Namefield will be greyed out.Click the button to provide the file path of the app to be added to kiosk mode.Notes:Ensure that the file path of the application is the same for all target devices associated with the policy.App Exit ActionDefines the action triggered when an app is closed or exited on a device.Default ActionDefine the action to be executed if an exit code, which is not mapped under theCustom App Exit Action, is returned upon app exit.Available Actions:None (Default): No action is taken.Restart Shell: Restarts the application.Restart Device: Restarts the device.Shutdown Device: Shuts down the device.Custom App Exit ActionClick Addto customize the app exit action. The admin can configure the action the device should take upon receiving a specific exit code from an app.Exit CodeThe exit code refers to the value returned by an application when it finishes execution. It is commonly used to indicate whether the program ran successfully or encountered an error. The range of exit codes typically spans from 0 to 255, with each code representing a specific result, which can vary between applications.For example:Exit Code 0: Typically indicates the app ran successfully without any errors.Exit ActionDetermines what the device should do based on the exit code received:None: No action is taken.Restart Shell: Restarts the app.Restart Device: Restarts the device.Shutdown Device: Powers off the device.Notes:Custom App Exit Actionis not supported on Windows Pro editions. For UWP/Windows Store App:Enter theKiosk Account Name.
