title: Troubleshooting
content: Probable Cause: The local Windows cache has not yet refreshed to reflect the newly applied UI restrictions. Solution: No immediate action is strictly required, as the restriction is already active. Even if a user clicks on the cached result, they will not be able to access the restricted setting (the Settings app will either open a blank page or redirect to the home screen). This visual inconsistency is temporary and will resolve itself once Windows naturally refreshes its background index. If an admin wants to clear it immediately, they can remotely initiate aRestart deviceaction to refresh the system s active cache. Probable Cause: The Settings app was running in the background during policy application. The device is running Windows Home edition, which does not support the SettingsPageVisibility CSP. Solution: The admin can execute a custom script to close the Settings app completely or initiate aRestart deviceaction from the Hexnode UEM portal to refresh the UI. Verify the OS edition inDevice Summary the device must be Pro, Enterprise, or Education.

title: Best Practices
content: Incremental Restriction Rollout:The admin should initially apply the policy to a small group of devices. This ensures that hiding a section like Accounts doesn t inadvertently block a user s ability to perform a business-critical task, such as adding a required Work/School account. Maintain Access to Time Language :For global organizations, the admin should consider leaving the Time Language section visible. This allows users to adjust their local time zone while traveling without requiring an IT ticket for a simple clock adjustment. Share on: Share on:

title: Customize Start menu of Windows devices
content: Help Center Managing Windows Devices Customizations Category filter

title: How to customize Windows Start menu from Hexnode
content: The Start on a Windows device gives users quick access to folders, power options, and account actions, all from a single, convenient location. To provide a smoother and more focused experience, IT administrators can customize start layouts using Hexnode UEM. With the Start policy, admins can add or hide shortcuts for folders, power options, and user account actions, showing users only what they need to see. This approach keeps the Start clean and intuitive, helping users find the right tools quickly. This policy is supported on: Windows 10 Pro, Enterprise, and Education editions. Windows 11 Pro, Enterprise, and Education editions.

title: Control the visibility of Start Menu shortcuts with Hexnode
content: Follow the below steps to customize start of Windows devices, Log in to your Hexnode UEM portal. Navigate to thePoliciestab. UnderDevice Policies, click onNew Policyto create a new one. Enter thePolicy NameandDescriptionin the provided fields.(or)Click on any existing policy to edit it. Navigate toWindows. Go toCustomizations Start . Click onConfigure.

title: Customizable Sections of the Windows Start Menu using Hexnode
content: When configuring the Start , admins can choose how shortcuts appear under Folder Shortcuts, Power , and User Tile . For each of the settings underFolder Shortcutssection, you can choose one of three options: Not enforced Default state no changes are applied. Hide shortcut Hides the shortcut from the Start . Show shortcut Displays the shortcut in the Start . Admins can control which folder shortcuts appear on the Start . For each folder, the following options are available: File Explorer:Displays a shortcut to open File Explorer, allowing users to browse drives, folders, and files. Settings:Shows a shortcut to the Windows Settings app for managing personalization, system preferences, and devices. Documents:Displays a shortcut to the user s Documents folder for storing and accessing personal or work files. Downloads:Displays a shortcut to the Downloads folder for quick access to recently downloaded files. Music:Shows a shortcut to the Music folder. Pictures:Displays a shortcut to the Pictures folder for managing and viewing stored images. Videos:Shows a shortcut to the Videos folder for accessing saved video files. Network:Displays the Network location in File Explorer to access shared computers, servers, and network devices. Personal folder:Displays a shortcut to the signed-in user s personal profile folder, including Desktop, Documents, and other subfolders. HomeGroup:Displays the HomeGroup option for sharing files, folders, and printers (supported only on Windows 10 v1703 to v1803). Admins can control which power options appear when users click the Power button in the Start . Power Button:Check the option to display the Power button on the Start . The following options are available only if the Power Button is enabled:Sleep:Adds the Sleep option to the power if checked hides it if unchecked.Hibernate:Adds the Hibernate option to the power if checked hides it if unchecked.Restart:Adds the Restart option to the power if checked hides it if unchecked.Shutdown:Adds the Shutdown option to the power if checked hides it if unchecked.Lock:Adds the Lock option to the power if checked hides it if unchecked. Sleep:Adds the Sleep option to the power if checked hides it if unchecked. Hibernate:Adds the Hibernate option to the power if checked hides it if unchecked. Restart:Adds the Restart option to the power if checked hides it if unchecked. Shutdown:Adds the Shutdown option to the power if checked hides it if unchecked. Lock:Adds the Lock option to the power if checked hides it if unchecked. Admins can customize the User Tile (the user profile icon) to control account-related options. User tile:Displays the user account tile on the Start . The following options are available only if the User Tile is enabled:Switch account:Adds the Switch account option if checked hides it if unchecked.Change account settings:Adds the Change account settings option if checked hides it if unchecked.Sign out:Adds the Sign out option if checked hides it if unchecked. Switch account:Adds the Switch account option if checked hides it if unchecked. Change account settings:Adds the Change account settings option if checked hides it if unchecked. Sign out:Adds the Sign out option if checked hides it if unchecked.

title: How to associate policies with devices?
content: If the policy has not yet been saved: Navigate toPolicy Targets. Select the target of the policy (Devices, Device Groups, Users, User Groups, Domain). Click on Add Devices. Select the devices you want to apply the policy to and clickOK. ClickSaveto apply the policies to the selected devices. If the policy has already been saved: Go to thePolicies tab. Select the policy you want to associate with devices. Click onManage Associate Targets. Select the devices or device groups to which you want to apply the policy. ClickAssociateto apply the policy to the selected devices.

title: What happens after associating the Start Menu policy?
content: Once the policy is associated with a device, shortcuts are added to the Start based on the configuration defined within the policy. For example, the policy may be configured to include shortcuts such as Documents, Personal Folder, File Explorer, and Settings, which appears on the Start .

title: Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
content: Does customizing the Start affect the actual files or system settings No. This policy only adds or hides shortcuts from the Start . It does not change or delete the underlying files, folders, or system settings. Do devices need to be restarted after applying Start customizations Yes, for changes to the Power Button and User Tile options, the device needs to berestarted. Why is the Hibernate option not visible on the Start for some devices The Hibernate option is only available on laptops. On desktops, this setting will not appear. 4. Can IT admins enforce a fixed Start layout with Hexnode s Start policy No. This policy is designed only to add or hide shortcuts for folders, power options, and user account actions. For enforcing a fixed layout, a separate Start layout policy would be required. If File Explorer is disabled on the Start , can users still access it Yes. Removing File Explorer from the Start only hides the shortcut. Users can still access it from other locations unless separately restricted.

title: Troubleshooting
content: Probable Cause: The device has successfully received the policy payload, but the Windows Shell (Explorer) remains cached and has not refreshed the UI. Visual elements like Power and User Tile modifications often require a session reset to initialize. Solution: Initiate a sign-out/sign-in cycle or a full system restart to force a shell refresh. For a non-intrusive fix, administrators can use the Hexnode UEM portal to remotely restart the device or guide the user to restart the Windows Explorer process within Task Manager. Probable Cause: Communication between the Hexnode UEM servers and the local Hexnode Agent has been interrupted. This is frequently caused by intermittent network connectivity, the device entering Battery Saver mode which kills background sync processes, or the Hexnode Agent service being suspended by the OS. Solution: Verify the device s internet stability and connect to a power source to disable background throttling. Open the Hexnode UEM app locally on the Windows device and trigger a manual Sync .

title: Best Practices
content: Verify OS Editions:Since this feature is exclusively supported on Windows Pro, Enterprise, and Education editions, regularly audit your device inventory. Exclude Windows Home edition devices from this policy target to avoid deployment errors. Communicate Changes to Users:If you plan to hide heavily used shortcuts (like the Settings app, File Explorer, or the Restart button), notify users in advance. Provide them with alternative methods to access these functions to prevent a spike in IT support tickets. Share on: Share on:

title: Windows device management: An edition-wise feature comparison list - Hexnode Help Center
content: Help Center Managing Windows Devices Edition wise feature comparison Category filter

title: Windows device management: An edition-wise feature comparison list
content: Microsoft offers various editions for Windows operating system, specifically tailored to meet different business use cases. Some editions of Windows are distributed on devices directly from a device manufacturer, while the other editions such as Enterprise and Education are made available only through volume licensing channels. Here s the complete list of Windows device management features in Hexnode UEM along with the Windows 10/11 editions they support.

title: Distribution Server for windows
content: Help Center Managing Windows Devices Hexnode Settings Category filter

title: Configure a distribution server for Windows
content: This documentation helps administrators configure distribution servers for Windows devices using the DAFS feature in Hexnode UEM. In environments where enterprise applications need to be deployed reliably and efficiently, Hexnode s Distributed Apps and Files Server provides a solution. Instead of each device individually connecting to Hexnode s server to download apps or updates, DAFS enables a local server within the office network to handle the enterprise app distribution. This method helps reduce internet usage and improves deployment speed, especially when managing devices across multiple locations. Setting up a DAFS involves provisioning a supported Windows device to act as the distribution server. Hexnode provides a setup package that automates the installation of Docker Desktop and pulls the necessary server components. Once configured, the DAFS syncs all enterprise apps automatically and begins serving them over the local network to devices associated with the same site. To ensure optimal performance, Hexnode provides a range of configurable options within the DAFS setup. These settings control how servers are prioritized and how apps are deployed when multiple DAF servers are associated with a single site.

title: Set up distribution server for Windows devices
content: To configure distribution servers, Login to the Hexnode UEM portal. Go toPolicies Device Policies, create a new policy or edit an already existing one. In the case of a new policy, add a policy name, which is mandatory before saving the policy, and an optional description. Navigate toWindows Hexnode Settings DAFS Configure. You will have the following settings to configure,

title: Configure separate configurations when the device is on and off site
content: When this option is enabled, administrators can apply different DAFS configurations for Windows devices depending on whether they are on-site or off-site. A device is consideredon-sitewhen its connection is within the range of public IP addresses configured for that site. A device is consideredoff-sitewhen its connection is outside the range of public IP addresses configured for that site. All the options listed below can be configured separately for both on-site and off-site Windows devices.

title: Download source preferences
content: Choose the source from which Windows devices will download content. The following options are available: Use DAFS when availableDevices will download content from the local DAF server whenever it is available. If the DAFS is unavailable, they will fetch content from the Hexnode cloud storage server. Devices will download content from the local DAF server whenever it is available. If the DAFS is unavailable, they will fetch content from the Hexnode cloud storage server. Use cloud storage servers onlyDevices will always download content directly from Hexnode s cloud storage server. When this option is selected, the Server Preference settings will not apply. Devices will always download content directly from Hexnode s cloud storage server. When this option is selected, the Server Preference settings will not apply. Use DAFS onlyDevices will download content exclusively from the local DAF server. If the server is unavailable, administrators may encounter an error when deploying content to those devices. Devices will download content exclusively from the local DAF server. If the server is unavailable, administrators may encounter an error when deploying content to those devices.

title: Server Preference
content: The Server Preference setting allows administrators to define which Distributed Apps and Files Servers (DAFS) should be prioritized when distributing enterprise apps and updates to Windows devices. When multiple DAF servers are associated with a site, this policy helps prioritize which servers should be used by the Windows devices targeted by the policy. Admins will have two options to choose from, Use the preference set in the associated siteWhen this option is selected, the DAFS preference defined under the associated site will be applied. You can configure this preference by navigating toAdmin Sites and DAFS Sites, selecting a site, and locating the DAFS section. Any DAF servers listed in that site configuration will be prioritized for devices associated with it. When this option is selected, the DAFS preference defined under the associated site will be applied. You can configure this preference by navigating toAdmin Sites and DAFS Sites, selecting a site, and locating the DAFS section. Any DAF servers listed in that site configuration will be prioritized for devices associated with it. Set a different preferenceThis option allows you to manually define a different DAFS preference. You can select specific DAF servers to be prioritized for the devices receiving the policy, regardless of the preference set under the associated site. When selected, this custom preference overrides the preference defined inAdmin Sites and DAFS Sitesfor the targeted devices. This option allows you to manually define a different DAFS preference. You can select specific DAF servers to be prioritized for the devices receiving the policy, regardless of the preference set under the associated site. When selected, this custom preference overrides the preference defined inAdmin Sites and DAFS Sitesfor the targeted devices.

title: Bandwidth settings
content: These options allow administrators to set restrictions on downloads from DAFS under certain conditions, such as exceeding bandwidth limits or when a device is connected to a VPN. Configure bandwidth restrictions Enable this option to define bandwidth restrictions for Windows devices using DAFS. Disallow cache server downloads on VPN Enable this option to prevent Windows devices from downloading content from the DAFS while connected to a VPN. If the settingUse DAFS onlyis selected underDownload source preferences, administrators may encounter an error when deploying content to the devices.

title: Associating the policy with target Windows devices
content: If the policy has not been saved, Navigate toPolicy Targets Devices Add Devices. Choose the target devices and clickOK. ClickSave. You can also associate the policy with device groups, users, user groups, or domains from the left pane of thePolicy Targetstab. If the policy has been saved, Go toPolicies. Choose the desired policy. Click on theManagedrop-down and selectAssociate Targets. Choose the target entities and clickAssociate. Share on: Share on:

title: Create user account on Windows devices enrolled in Hexnode UEM - Hexnode Help Center
content: Help Center Remote Actions Admin actions Category filter

title: Create user account on Windows devices enrolled in Hexnode UEM
content: The Create User Account remote action in Hexnode UEM allows IT administrators to provision local or directory-synced user profiles on Windows 10 and 11 endpoints over-the-air, streamlining employee onboarding and access management.

title: Overview of Remote User Provisioning
content: Remote account creation eliminates the need for manual setup on individual machines. By using the Hexnode console, administrators can define account roles, security credentials, and login behaviors for a distributed workforce from a single interface. Scalability:Provision accounts across multiple organizational units simultaneously. Security:Enforce complex passwords and specific account roles (Standard vs. Administrator). Efficiency:Automate data entry using wildcards for usernames and full names.

title: Prerequisites and Security Guidelines
content: Before executing the account creation action, ensure the following technical requirements and best practices are met:

title: Step-by-Step Guide: Creating a User Account
content: Administrators can choose to link an existing Hexnode user to the device or create a completely new local profile. Log in to theHexnode UEM portal. Navigate toManage Devicesand select the target device. Click onActions Policies Accounts Create User Account. Select one of the two configuration modes:

title: A. Choose User from Hexnode
content: Use this option to sync and map accounts already existing within the portal or integrated directory services. Domain:Select the specific local or directory domain. User:Choose the existing user from the drop-down. Auto-generated Fields:Account NameandFull Nameare populated automatically based on the user selection.

title: B. Create New User
content: Use this option for manual entry of local account details. Account Name:Specify the login name (supports wildcards). Full Name:Provide the display name (supports wildcards).

title: Common Account Configurations
content: Regardless of the mode selected, you must define the following: Description:Details visible under theLocal Accountstab in the portal. Account Role:SelectStandard(restricted access) orAdministrator(full system access). Password Management:Set and verify thePassword(supports wildcards). User must change password at login:Forces an immediate credential update. Password never expires:Bypasses standard password aging policies. User cannot change password:Prevents local modification of credentials. Password Hint:Text displayed upon incorrect login attempts (supports wildcards). Disable Account:Prevents the account from appearing on the login screen or Start . ClickCreate.

title: Device-End Experience
content: Once the action is successfully pushed and executed: The newUsernamebecomes visible on the Windows login screen. Administrators can verify the account locally by navigating toSettings Accounts. Hexnode tracks the account under theLocal Accountssub-tab of the specific device in the portal.

title: Can an Administrator account be created remotely?
content: Yes. You can select theAdministratorrole during the creation process to grant the user full elevated privileges on the Windows device.

title: What happens if wildcards are used?
content: Wildcards (e.g., username ) automatically pull data from the user s information already stored in Hexnode, ensuring the local account matches the portal records without manual typing.

title: Can multiple accounts be created at once?
content: While the action is triggered per device, you can select multiple devices in theManagetab to push theCreate User Accountaction in bulk.

title: Does “Password never expires” override local Group Policy?
content: Generally, MDM-set attributes for a specific account take precedence for that user, but it is recommended to align these settings with your organizationalGPO. Share on: Share on:

title: How to force BitLocker encryption on Windows with Hexnode UEM? - Hexnode Help Center
content: Help Center Remote Actions Admin actions Category filter

title: How to force BitLocker encryption on Windows with Hexnode UEM?
content: Force BitLocker Encryptionis a remote security action in Hexnode UEM that allows administrators to instantaneously trigger drive encryption on Windows devices using a TPM Startup PIN or a Fallback Password, bypassing the need for user-initiated setup.

title: Why Force BitLocker Encryption?
content: While a standard BitLocker policy defines the requirements for encryption, it often relies on the user to begin the process. TheForce BitLockeraction ensures immediate compliance by remotely encrypting system, fixed, and removable drives to protect sensitive data from unauthorized access or theft. Immediate Protection:Encrypts drives over-the-air without waiting for user action. Security for Stolen Devices:Renders data unreadable to intruders who lack the Startup PIN or Recovery Key. Customizable Scope:Allows administrators to target specific drives or only used disk space for efficiency.

title: Prerequisites and Critical Configuration
content: To ensure the remote action executes successfully, the following conditions must be met:

title: Step-by-Step Guide: Forcing BitLocker Encryption
content: Follow these steps to remotely encrypt Windows drives via the Hexnode portal: Log in to theHexnode UEM portal. Navigate to theManagetab and select the target device. Click onActions Security Force BitLocker Encryption. Configure theEncryption Parameters: Disk Space and Drive SelectionEncrypt used disk space:Recommended for new PCs encrypts only current data. New data is encrypted automatically thereafter.Encrypt entire drive:Recommended for existing PCs protects all data, including ly deleted files that could be retrieved by third-party tools.Drive Scope:ChooseEncrypt all drivesor specifyFixed,Removable, orSpecific Drives(separated by commas). Encrypt used disk space:Recommended for new PCs encrypts only current data. New data is encrypted automatically thereafter. Encrypt entire drive:Recommended for existing PCs protects all data, including ly deleted files that could be retrieved by third-party tools. Drive Scope:ChooseEncrypt all drivesor specifyFixed,Removable, orSpecific Drives(separated by commas). Authentication and RecoveryEnable auto unlock:Automatically unlocks fixed/removable drives when the OS drive is unlocked.TPM Startup PIN:Provide a 6-20 digit PIN. This is required every time the system reboots.Fallback Password:A minimum 8-character password for devices without a supported TPM.Mandate and escrow a recovery password:(Recommended) Ensures a recovery password is generated and stored in the Hexnode portal. Enable auto unlock:Automatically unlocks fixed/removable drives when the OS drive is unlocked. TPM Startup PIN:Provide a 6-20 digit PIN. This is required every time the system reboots. Fallback Password:A minimum 8-character password for devices without a supported TPM. Mandate and escrow a recovery password:(Recommended) Ensures a recovery password is generated and stored in the Hexnode portal. ClickProceed.

title: What Happens at the Device End?
content: Once the action is initiated: BitLocker is enabled based on the configurations in the associated policy (or default Windows settings if no policy exists). The drive begins the encryption process in the background. The user can verify status by navigating toControl Panel System and Security BitLocker Drive Encryption.

title: Where can the recovery password be found if a user forgets their PIN?
content: The escrowed recovery password can be viewed in the Hexnode portal underDevice Summary Hardware Infofor the specific device.

title: Can Hexnode retrieve the Startup PIN or Fallback Password?
content: No. Hexnode cannot retrieve the Startup PIN or Fallback Password once set. Administrators must record these securely at the time of execution. Only theRecovery Passwordis stored in the portal.

title: What happens if a drive that is already in use is encrypted?
content: It is recommended to selectEncrypt entire drivefor devices already in use to ensure that even deleted data fragments are protected from recovery tools.

title: Does the user need to be logged in for encryption to start?
content: The command is sent to the Hexnode Agent. While the encryption can begin in the background, the user will be required to enter theStartup PINorFallback Passwordupon the system reboot. Share on: Share on:

title: How to remotely Join/Unjoin AD domains on Windows devices? - Hexnode Help Center
content: Help Center Remote Actions Admin actions Category filter

title: How to remotely Join/Unjoin AD domains on Windows devices?
content: Active Directory (AD)management via Hexnode UEM allows administrators to bind user accounts to Windows endpoints, enabling seamless credential-based logins and centralized access to organizational network .

title: Understanding Active Directory Integration
content: Active Directory Domain Servicesstore directory information and manage user interactions with the domain, allowing users to log in to various devices using a single set of AD credentials. Centralized Identity:Eliminates the need to create separate local user accounts for every individual device. Seamless Access:Users can access network and log in to bound devices using their existing organizational credentials. Remote Management:Administrators can perform domain binding or removal over-the-air through the UEM console.

title: Technical Prerequisites
content: Before initiating a domain join or unjoin action, ensure the following requirements are met to maintain communication with theHexnode Agent:

title: Step-by-Step: Joining an AD Domain
content: Joining a domain binds a Windows device to a specific Active Directory controller, automating user account creation and enabling network resource access upon restart. Navigate to theManagetab in the Hexnode portal. Select the target Windows devices. Click onActions Groups Domains Join AD Domain. Configure the following parameters:Domain:Specify the exact domain name to which the device will be added.Server Address:Enter the full name of the domain controller hosting the target domain.Credentials:Provide theUsernameandPasswordfor the corresponding AD login.Restart Device:(Optional) Force an immediate restart to apply changes.Note:This may affect users with unsaved work. Domain:Specify the exact domain name to which the device will be added. Server Address:Enter the full name of the domain controller hosting the target domain. Credentials:Provide theUsernameandPasswordfor the corresponding AD login. Restart Device:(Optional) Force an immediate restart to apply changes.Note:This may affect users with unsaved work. This may affect users with unsaved work. ClickConfirmand complete the authentication using your portal credentials.

title: Verifying Domain Status
content: The Hexnode console provides real-time status updates in theDevice Summaryto confirm whether a device has successfully associated with a domain or workgroup. Navigate to theManagetab and select the specific device. Locate theDomain/Workgroup Infosection in theDevice Summarytab. Review the status fields:Domain Joined: Yes The device successfully joined a domain or workgroup.Domain Joined: No The device is not currently joined to a domain.Domain Joined: N/A The portal is unable to fetch the current AD status.Domain/Workgroup:Displays the name of the associated domain or workgroup. Domain Joined: Yes The device successfully joined a domain or workgroup. Domain Joined: No The device is not currently joined to a domain. Domain Joined: N/A The portal is unable to fetch the current AD status. Domain/Workgroup:Displays the name of the associated domain or workgroup.

title: Step-by-Step: Unjoining an AD Domain
content: Unjoining removes the device from the Active Directory environment, purging associated AD user accounts from the endpoint during the subsequent system restart. Navigate to theManagetab and select the devices. Click onActions Groups Domains Unjoin AD Domain. Provide theUsernameandPasswordfor the AD login credentials. SelectRestart Deviceto apply the removal immediately (ensure users have saved their work). ClickConfirmand provide your login credentials to deploy the configuration.

title: Does joining a domain automatically create a user account on the device?
content: Yes. Once a device joins the Active Directory Domain, the corresponding user account is automatically created on the endpoint.

title: Can users log in immediately after the “Join AD Domain” action is pushed?
content: The user can access the AD account using their credentials after the device restarts following policy association.

title: What happens to data when a device is unjoined?
content: When you unjoin a device, the AD account is removed from the device upon the restart, and the user can no longer access that specific AD account. Share on: Share on:

title: How to scan for Windows updates on devices from Hexnode? - Hexnode Help Center
content: Help Center Remote Actions Admin actions Category filter

title: How to scan for Windows updates on devices from Hexnode?
content: TheScan for Updatesremote action in Hexnode UEM allows IT administrators to remotely audit managed Windows devices for available OS and application patches, providing detailed visibility into update types, file sizes, and installation status.

title: Why Use “Scan for Updates”?
content: Maintaining up-to-date software is a cornerstone of endpoint security. This action enables administrators to identify critical vulnerabilities and missing patches from a centralized console without manual device inspection. By analyzing update attributes, IT teams can prioritize high-impact deployments to ensure fleet stability. Security Compliance:Identify missing security patches across Windows 10 and 11 fleets. Capacity Planning:ReviewDownload SizeandInstallation Sizeto manage network bandwidth and disk space. Version Control:Compare theInstalled Versionagainst theLatest Versionavailable via Windows Package Manager.

title: Step-by-Step Guide: Executing a Windows Update Scan
content: Follow these steps to trigger a real-time scan and view the results in the Hexnode portal. Log in to yourHexnode UEM portal. Navigate toManage Devicesand select the target Windows device. Click onActions Updates Scan for updates. Once the action is initiated, navigate to thePatches and Updatessub-tab on the device s summary page. TheLast Scannedtimestamp will update each time this action is successfully executed. If a scan has never succeeded, the portal will display the current OS version and a No updates available message.

title: Understanding Update Attributes
content: ThePatches and Updatessub-tab categorizes available software into a structured data table for administrative review.

title: Filtering and Sorting Updates
content: To streamline the management of large patch lists, administrators can filter results by the following criteria: All Updates:Comprehensive list of all detected software states. Installed Updates:History of successfully applied patches. Installable Updates:Critical list of missing updates ready for deployment. Failed Updates:Logs of updates that encountered errors during installation. Updates Pending Reboot:Updates that require a system restart to finalize.

title: Does this action automatically install the updates?
content: No. TheScan for updatesaction only identifies and lists available patches. To install them, you must use theInstall UpdatesorDeploy Patchesactions.

title: Where does Hexnode pull these updates from?
content: Hexnode queries the local Windows Update agent on the device, which retrieves information fromMicrosoft Update serversor theWindows Package Manager.

title: Can multiple devices be scanned at once?
content: Yes. You can select multiple Windows devices in theManagetab and execute theScan for updatesaction as a bulk command.

title: Why are some app updates appearing in the list?
content: Hexnode tracks both OS-level patches and application updates managed via the Windows environment, providing a unified view of the device s software health. Share on: Share on:

title: Executing custom scripts for Windows - Hexnode Help Center
content: Help Center Remote Actions Admin actions Category filter

title: Executing custom scripts for Windows
content: TheExecute Custom Scriptremote action allows IT administrators to automate routine system operations, software deployments, and configurations onWindowsendpoints by pushing scripts that execute automatically without manual intervention.

title: What is Windows Scripting in Hexnode?
content: Scripting utilizes command-line sequences to perform complex tasks not natively supported by the UEM interface. By deploying scripts over-the-air, organizations can ensure consistent system states and high operational efficiency across their entire fleet. Automation:Executes time-consuming tasks like server setup or mass installations automatically. Scalability:Pushes configurations to multiple PCs and tablets simultaneously. Flexibility:Supports advanced logic, including loops and conditionals, for sophisticated management.

title: Supported Scripting Languages
content: Hexnode UEM supports the primary scripting environments used in Windows administration.

title: Prerequisites and Constraints
content: Before deploying scripts, verify the following environmental and safety requirements:

title: Device Requirements
content: OS Support:Windows 10 (v1709 ) and Windows 11 PCs/tablets. Enrollment:Devices must be enrolled via theHexnode Installer appto ensure the latestHexnode Agent appis present.

title: Functional Constraints
content: No User Interaction:Scripts that require UI interactions (e.g., opening an app window, playing a video, or requiring user clicks) arenot supported. Naming Conventions:Filenames mustnotcontain: / : @ . Safety:Always validate scripts on a single test device before bulk deployment to avoid business continuity disruptions.

title: 1. Creating a Script File
content:
The simplest method is using theNotepadeditor on a Windows PC. For PowerShell (.ps1):Powershell ScriptNew-Item -Path D: temp Hexnode Folder -ItemType Directory1New-Item-Path D: temp Hexnode Folder -ItemTypeDirectory For Batch (.bat / .cmd):Bash Script@echo off
echo Hello C: Hexnode.txt12@echooffecho Hello C: Hexnode.txt

title: 2. Uploading to Hexnode Repository
content: Navigate to theContenttab in the Hexnode UEM portal. SelectMy Filesand clickAdd. Enter a file name and upload your .ps1, .bat, or .cmd file. ClickSave.

title: Executing the Remote Script
content: Follow these steps to deploy the script from the Hexnode console: Navigate toManage Devices. Select the target Windows PCs or tablets. SelectActions Others Execute Custom Script. ChooseWindowsas the platform. title: Supported Wildcards for Arguments
content: devicename , model , serialnumber , imei , osname , osversion , deviceid , phonenumber , name , email , username , domain , netbiosname , assettag , department , devicenotes , userprincipalname , alternateemail .

title: Troubleshooting Guides
content: Resolving Windows script failures often requires adjusting execution parameters, bypassing UI interaction blocks, ensuring TLS compatibility, or allowlisting management agents within security software (EDR).

title: 1. ‘Execute Custom Script’ remains in ‘In Progress’ state
content: Description:Scripts remain In progress for an extended period in theAction History. Cause:The script likely requires user or UI interaction, which the system blocks in the background. Solution:Use silent or non-interactive parameters. Including keywords like -silent, -passive, -quiet, or -nointeractive can allow cmdlets to run without prompts.

title: 2. PowerShell Internet Access Errors
content: Description:Errors such as Unable to download from URI when scripts try to fetch modules or files. Cause:PowerShell may not be using theTLS 1.2protocol required by modern web endpoints. Solution:Force the use of TLS 1.2 by adding this command to the start of your script:Powershell Internet access error script Net.ServicePointManager ::SecurityProtocol Net.SecurityProtocolType ::Tls121 Net.ServicePointManager ::SecurityProtocol Net.SecurityProtocolType ::Tls12

title: 3. Cmdlet/Function Not Recognized
content: Description:Commands that work in the local Terminal fail when executed via script. Cause:Certain commands requirePowerShell x64and fail in 32-bit environments. Solution:Include this snippet at the beginning of your script to force a relaunch in x64:cmdlet not recognized error scriptif ( PSHOME -like syswow64 ) Write-Output Relaunching as x64 (Join-Path ( PSHOME -replace syswow64 , sysnative ) powershell.exe) -File Script:MyInvocation.MyCommand.Path @args Exit 1if( PSHOME-like syswow64 ) Write-Output Relaunching as x64 (Join-Path( PSHOME-replace syswow64 , sysnative )powershell.exe)-File Script:MyInvocation.MyCommand.Path@argsExit

title: 4. ‘Unknown’ Action Status
content: Description:TheAction Historyshows Unknown and cannot fetch output. Cause:TheHexnode Agentwas restarted during execution (e.g., manual restart, third-party app trigger, or the script itself contained a restart command). Solution:Ensure the device is not restarted manually or by other apps until the script completes. If a restart is necessary, perform it as a separate action.

title: 5. Abrupt Termination (“Device must be awake and idle”)
content: Description:Execution fails with a termination error message. Cause:Issues include lack of internet, device inactivity, or blocking byEDR/Antivirussoftware. Solution:Verify the device is active and connected to Wi-Fi.Allowlistthe Hexnode agent (HexnodeAgent) in your EDR/Antivirus portal.Ensure the script has correct execution level parameters. Use currentUser if the script must run only for the logged-in user. Verify the device is active and connected to Wi-Fi. Allowlistthe Hexnode agent (HexnodeAgent) in your EDR/Antivirus portal. Ensure the script has correct execution level parameters. Use currentUser if the script must run only for the logged-in user.

title: Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
content: No. Hexnode does not support scripts that interact with the Device UI or require user interaction. All scripts must run in the background. Navigate toManage Devices Your Device Action History. Locate the script action and clickShow Output. This displays the result returned by the script. Hexnode supports standard PowerShell scripts however, success depends on the version available on the local OS. For newer modules, you may need to force TLS 1.2 as described in the troubleshooting section. The default is 30 minutes, but you can set a minimum of 15 minutes. If a script is complex (e.g., large software install), ensure the timeout is sufficient to prevent abrupt termination. Share on: Share on:

title: Windows Autopilot Zero-Touch Provisioning: Scaling 500k Devices
content: Help Center Solution Framework Hyper-Scale Provisioning Category filter

title: The Definitive Guide to Windows Autopilot Zero-Touch Provisioning
content: Welcome to the technical blueprint for achieving Zero-Touch deployment usingWindows AutopilotandHexnode UEM. This guide covers how to transition a Windows device from a shrink-wrapped box to a fully managed corporate asset without your IT department ever needing to physically touch the hardware. Unlike traditional imaging methods (like PXE or USB drives), Windows Autopilot does not re-install the operating system. Instead, it relies on aHardware Hash(a unique digital fingerprint) to register the device in the cloud.

title: The Device Lifecycle: From Factory to Employee
content: When an employee first powers on their new device and connects to Wi-Fi, the hardware identifies itself to theWindows Autopilot Deployment Service. The service then recognizes the device and redirects it toHexnode UEMfor automatic configuration. Here is the step-by-step technical handshake: Upload Hardware Hashes:Your OEM or Hardware Vendor uploads the device Hardware Hashes (PKID) to the Microsoft Intune Admin center. Sync Device IDs:Hexnode UEM syncs these Device IDs via the Microsoft Graph API. Assign Profiles:IT assigns a Deployment Profile (e.g., User-Driven or Self-Deploying) to the devices from the Microsoft Intune Admin center. Power On:The end-user receives the sealed box, powers on the device, and connects to Wi-Fi. Identification:The device asks Microsoft, Who am I by sending its Hardware Hash. Redirection:Microsoft responds, You belong to Your Enterprise . Redirecting to Hexnode. Enrollment:The device requests enrollment into Hexnode via Entra ID Authentication. Provisioning:Hexnode pushes all required apps, security policies, and certificates to the device. Ready:The user is greeted with a ready-to-use desktop.

title: Choosing an Autopilot Deployment Profile
content: The behavior of the device during the Out-of-Box Experience (OOBE) is governed by the profile you assign in Hexnode.

title: The Enrollment Status Page (ESP)
content: To ensure a device is secure before the user can access the desktop, Hexnode enforces theEnrollment Status Page (ESP). This acts as a holding pattern governed by three logical steps: SENSE (Device Preparation):The device downloads its assigned Autopilot Deployment profile configured in the Intune Admin Center and joins Entra ID. THINK (Account Setup):Hexnode checks the configuration for Blocking Apps these are critical security software (like your Antivirus or VPN) that must be present before the user is allowed to work. ACT (Enforcement):The device remains locked on the ESP screen until all Blocking Apps and mandatory profiles are successfully installed.

title: Pre-Provisioning (“White Glove” Service)
content: If you have remote employees with slow home internet connections, downloading heavy applications during the OOBE can result in a poor first-day experience. To solve this, IT can utilizePre-Provisioning. The Action:An IT technician unboxes the laptop in the office, boots it to the first OOBE screen, and taps theWindows Key 5 times. The Result:This triggers the device to download all heavy apps, policies, and updates using the corporate network. The Handoff:Once finished, IT reseals the device and ships it to the user. When the user receives it, they only need to perform a quick 2-minute final sign-in.

title: How to enable Lost Mode for Windows PCs - Hexnode Help Center
content: Help Center Remote Actions Admin actions Category filter

title: How to enable Lost Mode for Windows PCs
content: Lost Modeis a remote security action that locks a Windows 10 or 11 device to prevent unauthorized data access, displaying a custom recovery message while restricting all system functionalities until disabled by an administrator.

title: Why Enable Lost Mode?
content: If a corporate laptop is misplaced or stolen, sensitive data becomes vulnerable. Enabling Lost Mode ensures that even if an intruder has physical possession, the system is inaccessible. Data Lockdown:Restricts all device operations to prevent data breaches. Device Recovery:Displays contact information and custom messages to the finder. Remote Control:The device remains locked even after a restart and can only be unlocked via the Hexnode portal.

title: Prerequisites and Compatibility
content: Before initiating Lost Mode, ensure the following technical requirements are met: If theEnable Lost Modeaction is greyed out, ensure theHexnode UEM appis updated to the latest version on the endpoint.

title: Step-by-Step Guide: Enabling Lost Mode
content: Follow these steps to secure a reported lost or stolen Windows device: Log in to theHexnode UEM console. Navigate toManage Devicesand select the target device. Click onActions Security Enable Lost Mode. Configure the display information:Custom Message:Provide instructions for the finder.Phone Number:Enter a contact number (supports the phonenumber wildcard).Footnote:Add additional recovery details. Custom Message:Provide instructions for the finder. Phone Number:Enter a contact number (supports the phonenumber wildcard). Footnote:Add additional recovery details. ClickEnable.

title: Supported Wildcards for Custom Messages
content: You can use the following wildcards to dynamically populate device-specific info on the lock screen: devicename , deviceid , username , model , udid , assettag , serialnumber , name , department , imei , email , devicenotes , alternateemail , phonenumber , domain , osname , wifimacaddress , osversion , iccid , userprincipalname , netbiosname , newline .

title: Tracking and Recovering the Device
content: Hexnode allows administrators to monitor the movement of a lost device through integrated location services. Location Tracking Policy:If associated before the device was lost, Hexnode fetches the location at periodic intervals. Scan Device Location:Execute this action to retrieve the immediate, real-time coordinates of the device. Viewing Data:Access coordinates viaDevice Summary Recent Locationor theLocation Historysub-tab.

title: Disabling Lost Mode
content: The device remains unusable until the administrator explicitly lifts the restriction. In the Hexnode portal, go toManage. Select the recovered device. Click onActions Security Disable Lost Mode. Disenrolling a device while it is in Lost Mode will disable the lock but will also remove all remote management capabilities.

title: Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
content: Yes, the Windows device will restart immediately after the command is executed to ensure the lockdown environment is initialized. No. The device will continue to boot into the Lost Mode screen showing your custom message and phone number until the action is disabled from the Hexnode portal. InSingle-app Kiosk, the account locks upon restart but can be recovered by disabling Lost Mode. InMulti-app Kiosk, you must ensure the Hexnode UEM app is allowlisted in the kiosk policy to ensure the Lost Mode interface functions correctly. Share on: Share on:

title: Single Pane of Glass: Unified Windows, Mac & Linux Management
content: Help Center Solution Framework Hexnode s Unified Management Vision Category filter

title: The Challenge: The “Three-Console” Headache
content: For years, IT administrators have been trapped in a siloed management cycle. Managing Windows required one tool, macOS required another, and Linux if managed at all often required manual scripting or a third niche console. This fragmentation leads to: Console Fatigue:Constantly switching tabs and re-authenticating across different platforms. Inconsistent Security:A High security policy on Windows might not look the same as one on a Mac. Delayed Response:Troubleshooting takes longer when data is scattered across three different databases.

title: The Solution: Hexnode’s “Single Pane of Glass”
content: In 2026, Unified isn t just a buzzword it s an operational necessity. Hexnode UEM provides a centralized command center where the underlying OS becomes secondary to the management goal. Whether you are pushing a patch, enforcing encryption, or troubleshooting a remote device, the workflow remains identical.

title: 2026 Feature Spotlight: Beyond Just “Visibility”
content: A true single pane of glass in 2026 does more than just show you the devices it manages the complexity for you.

title: 1. Hexnode Genie AI: Unified Troubleshooting
content: Instead of digging through Windows Event Viewer or Linux system logs, Genie AI correlates telemetry from all three platforms. If a Mac user in London and a Linux user in Berlin both experience VPN drops, Genie AI identifies the common configuration error across both platforms and suggests a one-click fix.

title: 2. Universal Policy Engine
content: Hexnode allows you to create Dynamic Groups that aren t restricted by OS. You can create a group called Finance Team that automatically applies a BitLocker policy to the Windows users and a FileVault policy to the Mac users within the same logical workflow.

title: 3. Automated Patching (Self-Healing)
content: Our 2026 engine treats patches as a single Health Score. From your console, you can set a global rule: No device, regardless of OS, can access corporate mail if it is more than 2 patches behind. Hexnode handles the different update architectures of Windows Update, Apple s Software Update, and Linux apt/dnf in the background.

title: How to Get Started?
content: Navigate to the Manage Tab:See your entire fleet (Windows, Mac, Linux) in a single list. Filter by Compliance:Instantly see which devices are Non-Compliant, regardless of their OS. Deploy a Unified Policy:Go toPolicies New Policyand select the restrictions you want to apply across your multi-OS environment. Still jumping between consoles Experience the Holy Grail of IT management with a personalized walkthrough. Schedule a Demo with a Hexnode Expert Share on: Share on:

Enable USB Debugging on your device (Developer Options USB Debugging). The device must not be locked and should be active. On your Windows Computer, download andInstallAndroid SDK. Once the installation completes, Android SDK will launch automatically. Select and Install Android Platform Tools. Connect your Android device to your Windows PC via USB cable. Open a new command prompt session with administrator privileges, change the working directory to the path where Android SDK is installed (cd path where Android SDK resides /platform-tools). First, run the following commandadb.exe kill-server1adb.exekill-server , run the command to list the devices connected to your PC. Note down the device id of your device. Run,adb.exe logcat -v threadtime device id C: android-debug.log1adb.exelogcat-vthreadtime deviceid C: android-debug.logEnter the device id obtained in the step in place of device id . Perform any actions on your Android device. To stop the adb process, pressCtrl Con the command prompt window. The debug data can be viewed from c: android-debug.log file. In case your machine does not recognize your device, try the following: Unplug and replug your device. EnableVerify apps over USBoption (Settings System Developer Options Debugging). Check if the device notification bar displays the messageUSB debugging connected. SelectRevoke USB debugging authorizationsto revoke access to all computers you ve ly authorized (Settings System Developer Options Debugging).

title: Using Mac
content: Ensure that the Developer options is enabled on your Android device (Settings Device Tap on Build Number 7 times and Developer Options will be visible in your Settings ). Enable USB Debugging on your device (Developer Options USB Debugging). The device must not be locked and should be active. On your Mac device, download andInstallAndroid SDK. On your Mac device, download and install Android SDK. Once the installation completes, Android SDK will launch automatically. Select and Install Android Platform Tools. Connect your Android device to your Mac device via USB cable. First, run the following command./adb kill-server1./adbkill-server , run the command to list the devices connected to your PC. Note down the device id of your device.
